“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog|archive-date=2023-01-26|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美}} and Mi (彌、見、美、海、看).
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
More people with it in their names are, Suga no Yatsumimi, {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|lt=Futimi no Kami}}, {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Torimimi|ja|鳥取神}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神}} is also considered a regional chief.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
Some people have names historians think came from the place they ruled, plus Mi or Mimi. People like this include Ikatomi of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Ika District, Shiga|lt=Ika District|en}}, [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] (Ikatomi, perhaps {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命}} ). [[Emperor Suizei]] 's Japanese-style name, Kaunumakawamimi, and his older brother, [[Kamuyaimimi]] may come from places called Kaunumakawa and Kamuyai.
The leader of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Toumakoku|en}} was called Mimi.<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|last=第２版,世界大百科事典内言及|first=百科事典マイペディア,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典|title=投馬国とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%8A%95%E9%A6%AC%E5%9B%BD-104202|access-date=2022-06-21|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
== Mimi and Mi at shrines ==
"Mimi" and "Mi" are also present in the names of several shrines across Japan such as the {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社}} in [[Wakasa Province]] and {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en}} in [[Shinano Province]]. * {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社}} in [[Wakasa Province]],
* {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en}} in [[Shinano Province]]
* {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine|ja|阿志都弥神社}} in [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] * {{ill|Kayanarumi Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美神社}} in Takaichi County, [[Yamato Province]] * {{ill|Haniyamahimi Shrine|ja|波爾移麻比彌神社}} in {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Mima District, Tokushima|lt=Mima District|en}} [[Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]]
== Mimi and Mi in clans == Furthermore, these words appear in the names of ancient Japanese clans, these are some of those clans.
* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari}}. * {{ill|Wanizumi clan|ja|和珥氏}}, which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kamo clan|en}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏}} and {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|知々夫国造}} - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Nukatabe clan|ja|額田部氏}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en}}</ref>
* {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Tagishimimi|en}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").
=== Genealogy === Note that [[Emperor Suizei]] went by the original name of ''Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto'' {{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}

The leader of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Toumakoku|en}} was called Mimi.<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|last=第２版,世界大百科事典内言及|first=百科事典マイペディア,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典|title=投馬国とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%8A%95%E9%A6%AC%E5%9B%BD-104202|access-date=2022-06-21|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
== Mimi and Mi at shrines ==
"Mimi" and "Mi" are also present in the names of several shrines across Japan such as the {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|12=draft}} in [[Wakasa Province]] and {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|12=draft}} in [[Shinano Province]]. * {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|12=draft}} in [[Wakasa Province]],
* {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|12=draft}} in [[Shinano Province]]
* {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine|ja|阿志都弥神社|12=draft}} in [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] * {{ill|Kayanarumi Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美神社|12=draft}} in Takaichi County, [[Yamato Province]] * {{ill|Haniyamahimi Shrine|ja|波爾移麻比彌神社|12=draft}} in {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Mima District, Tokushima|lt=Mima District|en}} [[Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]]
== Mimi and Mi in clans == Furthermore, these words appear in the names of ancient Japanese clans, these are some of those clans.
* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=draft}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari}}. * {{ill|Wanizumi clan|ja|和珥氏|12=draft}}, which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=draft}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kamo clan|en}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|12=draft}} and {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|知々夫国造}} - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=draft}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|12=draft}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Nukatabe clan|ja|額田部氏}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|12=draft}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en|12=draft}}</ref>
* {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Tagishimimi|en}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").
=== Genealogy === Note that [[Emperor Suizei]] went by the original name of ''Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto'' {{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}

{{draft categories| [[Category:History of Japan]] [[Category:Japanese words and phrases]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:Mimi and Mi]] [[sn:Mimi and Mi]] [[fr:Mimi et Mi]] [[ja:ミミおよびミ]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mimi and Mi}}

“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog|archive-date=2023-01-26|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|12=draft}} and Mi (彌、見、美、海、看).
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
More people with it in their names are, Suga no Yatsumimi, {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|lt=Futimi no Kami|12=draft}}, {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Torimimi|ja|鳥取神}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|12=draft}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=draft}} is also considered a regional chief.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
Some people have names historians think came from the place they ruled, plus Mi or Mimi. People like this include Ikatomi of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Ika District, Shiga|lt=Ika District|en}}, [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] (Ikatomi, perhaps {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=draft}} ). [[Emperor Suizei]] 's Japanese-style name, Kaunumakawamimi, and his older brother, [[Kamuyaimimi]] may come from places called Kaunumakawa and Kamuyai.
The leader of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Toumakoku|en}} was called Mimi.<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|last=第２版,世界大百科事典内言及|first=百科事典マイペディア,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典|title=投馬国とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%8A%95%E9%A6%AC%E5%9B%BD-104202|access-date=2022-06-21|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
== Mimi and Mi at shrines ==
"Mimi" and "Mi" are also present in the names of several shrines across Japan such as the {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|12=draft}} in [[Wakasa Province]] and {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|12=draft}} in [[Shinano Province]]. * {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|12=draft}} in [[Wakasa Province]],
* {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|12=draft}} in [[Shinano Province]]
* {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine|ja|阿志都弥神社|12=draft}} in [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] * {{ill|Kayanarumi Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美神社|12=draft}} in Takaichi County, [[Yamato Province]] * {{ill|Haniyamahimi Shrine|ja|波爾移麻比彌神社|12=draft}} in {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Mima District, Tokushima|lt=Mima District|en}} [[Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]]
== Mimi and Mi in clans == Furthermore, these words appear in the names of ancient Japanese clans, these are some of those clans.
* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=draft}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari}}. * {{ill|Wanizumi clan|ja|和珥氏|12=draft}}, which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=draft}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kamo clan|en}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|12=draft}} and {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|知々夫国造}} - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=draft}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|12=draft}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Nukatabe clan|ja|額田部氏}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|12=draft}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en|12=draft}}</ref>
* {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Tagishimimi|en}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").

Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|12=draft}} and Mi (彌、見、美、海、看).
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
More people with it in their names are, Suga no Yatsumimi, {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|lt=Futimi no Kami|12=draft}}, {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Torimimi|ja|鳥取神}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|12=draft}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=draft}} is also considered a regional chief.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
Some people have names historians think came from the place they ruled, plus Mi or Mimi. People like this include Ikatomi of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Ika District, Shiga|lt=Ika District|en}}, [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] (Ikatomi, perhaps {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=draft}} ). [[Emperor Suizei]] 's Japanese-style name, Kaunumakawamimi, and his older brother, [[Kamuyaimimi]] may come from places called Kaunumakawa and Kamuyai.
The leader of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Toumakoku|en}} was called Mimi.<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|last=第２版,世界大百科事典内言及|first=百科事典マイペディア,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典|title=投馬国とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%8A%95%E9%A6%AC%E5%9B%BD-104202|access-date=2022-06-21|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
== Mimi and Mi at shrines ==
"Mimi" and "Mi" are also present in the names of several shrines across Japan such as the {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|12=draft}} in [[Wakasa Province]] and {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|12=draft}} in [[Shinano Province]]. * {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|12=draft}} in [[Wakasa Province]],
* {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|12=draft}} in [[Shinano Province]]
* {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine|ja|阿志都弥神社|12=draft}} in [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] * {{ill|Kayanarumi Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美神社|12=draft}} in Takaichi County, [[Yamato Province]] * {{ill|Haniyamahimi Shrine|ja|波爾移麻比彌神社|12=draft}} in {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Mima District, Tokushima|lt=Mima District|en}} [[Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]]
== Mimi and Mi in clans == Furthermore, these words appear in the names of ancient Japanese clans, these are some of those clans.
* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=draft}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari}}. * {{ill|Wanizumi clan|ja|和珥氏|12=draft}}, which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=draft}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kamo clan|en}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|12=draft}} and {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|知々夫国造}} - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=draft}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|12=draft}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Nukatabe clan|ja|額田部氏}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|12=draft}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en|12=draft}}</ref>
* {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Tagishimimi|en}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").
=== Genealogy === Note that [[Emperor Suizei]] went by the original name of ''Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto'' {{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}
== References == {{Reflist}}

“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog|archive-date=2023-01-26|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|12=draft}} and Mi (彌、見、美、海、看).
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
More people with it in their names are, Suga no Yatsumimi, {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|lt=Futimi no Kami|12=draft}}, {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Torimimi|ja|鳥取神}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|12=draft}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=draft}} is also considered a regional chief.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
Some people have names historians think came from the place they ruled, plus Mi or Mimi. People like this include Ikatomi of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Ika District, Shiga|lt=Ika District|en}}, [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] (Ikatomi, perhaps {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=draft}} ). [[Emperor Suizei]] 's Japanese-style name, Kaunumakawamimi, and his older brother, [[Kamuyaimimi]] may come from places called Kaunumakawa and Kamuyai.
The leader of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Toumakoku|en}} was called Mimi.<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|last=第２版,世界大百科事典内言及|first=百科事典マイペディア,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典|title=投馬国とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%8A%95%E9%A6%AC%E5%9B%BD-104202|access-date=2022-06-21|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
== Mimi and Mi at shrines ==
"Mimi" and "Mi" are also present in the names of several shrines across Japan such as the {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|12=draft}} in [[Wakasa Province]] and {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|12=draft}} in [[Shinano Province]]. * {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|12=draft}} in [[Wakasa Province]],
* {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|12=draft}} in [[Shinano Province]]
* {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine|ja|阿志都弥神社|12=draft}} in [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] * {{ill|Kayanarumi Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美神社|12=draft}} in Takaichi County, [[Yamato Province]] * {{ill|Haniyamahimi Shrine|ja|波爾移麻比彌神社|12=draft}} in {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Mima District, Tokushima|lt=Mima District|en}} [[Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]]
== Mimi and Mi in clans == Furthermore, these words appear in the names of ancient Japanese clans, these are some of those clans.
* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=draft}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari}}. * {{ill|Wanizumi clan|ja|和珥氏|12=draft}}, which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=draft}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kamo clan|en}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|12=draft}} and {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|知々夫国造}} - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=draft}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|12=draft}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Nukatabe clan|ja|額田部氏}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|12=draft}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en|12=draft}}</ref>
* {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Tagishimimi|en}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").
=== Genealogy === Note that [[Emperor Suizei]] went by the original name of ''Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto'' {{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog|archive-date=2023-01-26|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|12=draft}} and Mi (彌、見、美、海、看).
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
More people with it in their names are, Suga no Yatsumimi, {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|lt=Futimi no Kami|12=draft}}, {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Torimimi|ja|鳥取神}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|12=draft}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=draft}} is also considered a regional chief.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
Some people have names historians think came from the place they ruled, plus Mi or Mimi. People like this include Ikatomi of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Ika District, Shiga|lt=Ika District|en}}, [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] (Ikatomi, perhaps {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=draft}} ). [[Emperor Suizei]] 's Japanese-style name, Kaunumakawamimi, and his older brother, [[Kamuyaimimi]] may come from places called Kaunumakawa and Kamuyai.
The leader of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Toumakoku|en}} was called Mimi.<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|last=第２版,世界大百科事典内言及|first=百科事典マイペディア,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典|title=投馬国とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%8A%95%E9%A6%AC%E5%9B%BD-104202|access-date=2022-06-21|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
== Mimi and Mi at shrines ==
"Mimi" and "Mi" are also present in the names of several shrines across Japan such as the {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|12=draft}} in [[Wakasa Province]] and {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|12=draft}} in [[Shinano Province]]. * {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|12=draft}} in [[Wakasa Province]],
* {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|12=draft}} in [[Shinano Province]]
* {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine|ja|阿志都弥神社|12=draft}} in [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] * {{ill|Kayanarumi Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美神社|12=draft}} in Takaichi County, [[Yamato Province]] * {{ill|Haniyamahimi Shrine|ja|波爾移麻比彌神社|12=draft}} in {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Mima District, Tokushima|lt=Mima District|en}} [[Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]]
== Mimi and Mi in clans == Furthermore, these words appear in the names of ancient Japanese clans, these are some of those clans.
* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=draft}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari}}. * {{ill|Wanizumi clan|ja|和珥氏|12=draft}}, which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=draft}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kamo clan|en}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|12=draft}} and {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|知々夫国造}} - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=draft}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|12=draft}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Nukatabe clan|ja|額田部氏}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|12=draft}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en|12=draft}}</ref>
* {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Tagishimimi|en}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").
=== Genealogy === Note that [[Emperor Suizei]] went by the original name of ''Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto'' {{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}
== References == {{Reflist}}

* {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kamo clan|en}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|12=draft}} and {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|知々夫国造}} - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=draft}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|12=draft}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Nukatabe clan|ja|額田部氏}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|12=draft}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en|12=draft}}</ref>
* {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Tagishimimi|en}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").
=== Genealogy === Note that [[Emperor Suizei]] went by the original name of ''Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto'' {{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}

revision.text:
“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog|archive-date=2023-01-26|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|12=draft|12=draft}} and Mi (彌、見、美、海、看).
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
More people with it in their names are, Suga no Yatsumimi, {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|lt=Futimi no Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Torimimi|ja|鳥取神}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|12=draft|12=draft}} is also considered a regional chief.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
Some people have names historians think came from the place they ruled, plus Mi or Mimi. People like this include Ikatomi of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Ika District, Shiga|lt=Ika District|en}}, [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] (Ikatomi, perhaps {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=draft|12=draft}} ). [[Emperor Suizei]] 's Japanese-style name, Kaunumakawamimi, and his older brother, [[Kamuyaimimi]] may come from places called Kaunumakawa and Kamuyai.
The leader of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Toumakoku|en}} was called Mimi.<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|last=第２版,世界大百科事典内言及|first=百科事典マイペディア,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典|title=投馬国とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%8A%95%E9%A6%AC%E5%9B%BD-104202|access-date=2022-06-21|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
== Mimi and Mi at shrines ==
"Mimi" and "Mi" are also present in the names of several shrines across Japan such as the {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in [[Wakasa Province]] and {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|12=draft|12=draft}} in [[Shinano Province]]. * {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in [[Wakasa Province]],
* {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|12=draft|12=draft}} in [[Shinano Province]]
* {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine|ja|阿志都弥神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] * {{ill|Kayanarumi Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Takaichi County, [[Yamato Province]] * {{ill|Haniyamahimi Shrine|ja|波爾移麻比彌神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Mima District, Tokushima|lt=Mima District|en}} [[Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]]
== Mimi and Mi in clans == Furthermore, these words appear in the names of ancient Japanese clans, these are some of those clans.
* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=draft|12=draft}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari}}. * {{ill|Wanizumi clan|ja|和珥氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=draft|12=draft}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kamo clan|en}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|知々夫国造}} - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=draft|12=draft}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|12=draft|12=draft}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Nukatabe clan|ja|額田部氏}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|12=draft|12=draft}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en|12=draft|12=draft}}</ref>
* {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Tagishimimi|en}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").
=== Genealogy === Note that [[Emperor Suizei]] went by the original name of ''Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto'' {{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}
== References == {{Reflist}}

People like this include Ikatomi of {{ill|Ika District, Shiga|lt=Ika District|en}}, [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] (Ikatomi, perhaps {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|12=draft|12=draft}} ). [[Emperor Suizei]] 's Japanese-style name, Kaunumakawamimi, and his older brother, [[Kamuyaimimi]] may come from places called Kaunumakawa and Kamuyai.
The leader of {{ill|Toumakoku|en}} was called Mimi.<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|last=第２版,世界大百科事典内言及|first=百科事典マイペディア,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典|title=投馬国とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%8A%95%E9%A6%AC%E5%9B%BD-104202|access-date=2022-06-21|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
== Mimi and Mi at shrines ==
"Mimi" and "Mi" are also present in the names of several shrines across Japan such as the {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in [[Wakasa Province]] and {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|12=draft|12=draft}} in [[Shinano Province]]. * {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in [[Wakasa Province]],
* {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|12=draft|12=draft}} in [[Shinano Province]]
* {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine|ja|阿志都弥神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] * {{ill|Kayanarumi Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Takaichi County, [[Yamato Province]] * {{ill|Haniyamahimi Shrine|ja|波爾移麻比彌神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Mima District, Tokushima|lt=Mima District|en}} [[Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]]
== Mimi and Mi in clans == Furthermore, these words appear in the names of ancient Japanese clans, these are some of those clans.
* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=draft|12=draft}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari}}. * {{ill|Wanizumi clan|ja|和珥氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=draft|12=draft}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{ill|Kamo clan|en}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|知々夫国造}} - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=draft|12=draft}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|12=draft|12=draft}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{ill|Nukatabe clan|ja|額田部氏}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|12=draft|12=draft}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en|12=draft|12=draft}}</ref>
* {{ill|Tagishimimi|en}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").
=== Genealogy === Note that [[Emperor Suizei]] went by the original name of ''Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto'' {{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}

<!--Mimi and Mi-->

“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog|archive-date=2023-01-26|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美}} and Mi (彌、見、美、海、看).
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
More people with it in their names are, Suga no Yatsumimi, {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|lt=Futimi no Kami}}, {{ill|Torimimi|ja|鳥取神}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神}} is also considered a regional chief.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
Some people have names historians think came from the place they ruled, plus Mi or Mimi. People like this include Ikatomi of {{ill|Ika District, Shiga|lt=Ika District|en}}, [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] (Ikatomi, perhaps {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命}} ). [[Emperor Suizei]] 's Japanese-style name, Kaunumakawamimi, and his older brother, [[Kamuyaimimi]] may come from places called Kaunumakawa and Kamuyai.
The leader of {{ill|Toumakoku|en}} was called Mimi.<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|last=第２版,世界大百科事典内言及|first=百科事典マイペディア,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典|title=投馬国とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%8A%95%E9%A6%AC%E5%9B%BD-104202|access-date=2022-06-21|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
== Mimi and Mi at shrines ==
"Mimi" and "Mi" are also present in the names of several shrines across Japan such as the {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社}} in [[Wakasa Province]] and {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en}} in [[Shinano Province]]. * {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社}} in [[Wakasa Province]],
* {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en}} in [[Shinano Province]]
* {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine|ja|阿志都弥神社}} in [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] * {{ill|Kayanarumi Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美神社}} in Takaichi County, [[Yamato Province]] * {{ill|Haniyamahimi Shrine|ja|波爾移麻比彌神社}} in {{ill|Mima District, Tokushima|lt=Mima District|en}} [[Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]]
== Mimi and Mi in clans == Furthermore, these words appear in the names of ancient Japanese clans, these are some of those clans.
* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari}}. * {{ill|Wanizumi clan|ja|和珥氏}}, which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{ill|Kamo clan|en}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏}} and {{ill|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|知々夫国造}} - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{ill|Nukatabe clan|ja|額田部氏}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en}}</ref>
* {{ill|Tagishimimi|en}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").
=== Genealogy === Note that [[Emperor Suizei]] went by the original name of ''Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto'' {{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}

revision.comment: 35 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:ミミおよびミ up to rev 103153801
revision.text:
'''ミミ'''（耳、彌彌、美美）および'''ミ'''（彌、見、美、海、看）は古代[[日本]]の[[尊称]]。
== 概説 == 「ミ」（霊）は神格を表す尊称（神霊の意）として「[[ワタツミ|ワタツ'''ミ''']]」（海つ霊）や「[[オオヤマツミ|ヤマツ'''ミ''']]」（山つ霊）等と用いられ、「ミミ」（霊霊）はその「ミ」（霊）を重ねたものとされる<ref>[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>。
== 地域的首長としてのミミおよびミ == 『[[魏志倭人伝]]』は[[3世紀]]の[[投馬国]]の首長に「彌彌（ミミ）」および「彌彌那利（ミミナリ）」がいたことを記している。
『[[古事記]]』および『[[日本書紀]]』では[[和泉|和泉地方]]に[[陶津耳]]（スエツミミ）、[[摂津国|摂津地方]]に[[三嶋湟咋|三嶋溝{{lang|zh|橛}}耳]]（ミシマミゾクイミミ）、[[丹波国|丹波地方]]に玖賀耳（クガミミ）、また[[但馬国|但馬地方]]に前津耳（マサキツミミ）が記録されているが、いずれもその地方の首長と考えられている。 『[[出雲国風土記]]』には[[波多都美命]]（ハタツミ）や伎自麻都美（キジマツミ）など「ミ」の付く人物が記されており、いずれも地域的首長である<ref>溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973年 - 1974年。波多都美は[[出雲国]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美は出雲国飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref>。
また『古事記』の出雲神話に出てくる[[アシナヅチ・テナヅチ|須賀之八耳]]（スガノヤツミミ）、[[布帝耳神|布帝耳]]（フテミミ）、[[鳥取神|鳥耳]]（トリミミ）、[[多比理岐志麻流美神|多比理岐志麻流美]]（タヒリキシマルミ）、[[天日腹大科度美神|天日腹大科度美]]（アメノヒバラオオシナドミ）も地域的首長と考えられる<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>。
他に[[近江国]][[伊香郡]]の伊香刀美（イカトミ、[[伊香津臣命]]か）なども地名にミを付けた地域的首長を表すと考えられる。また[[綏靖天皇]]の和風諱号である神沼河耳命（カムヌナカワミミ）や、その兄である[[神八井耳命]]（カムヤイミミ）なども同様である。
== 神社におけるミミおよびミ == [[若狭国]]の[[彌美神社|彌美（ミミ）神社]]、[[信濃国]]の[[諏訪大社|南方刀美（ミナカタトミ）神社]]、[[近江国]]の[[阿志都弥神社|阿志都弥（アシツミ）神社]]、[[大和国]][[高市郡]]の[[加夜奈留美神社|加夜奈留美（カヤナルミ）神社]]、[[阿波国]][[美馬郡]]の[[波爾移麻比彌神社|波尓移麻比彌（ハニヤマヒミ）神社]]などは、ミミやミを神名に入れた神社である。
== 姓氏の中のミミおよびミ == [[氏]]の名の末尾にミ（ツミ）がつく代表的な例には、[[綿積豊玉彦命]]（ワダツミ）を祖先とする[[阿曇氏]]（アヅミ）や[[饒速日命]]を祖先とする[[穂積氏]]（ホヅミ）、[[和珥氏]]の同族と見られる鰐積氏（ワニヅミ）、[[出雲国造]]の同族と見られる出雲積氏（イヅモヅミ）、津積氏（ツヅミ）がある。
氏の名にミは付かないが、その祖先にミがつく例には、[[賀茂朝臣氏|賀茂氏]]の祖先の[[大賀茂都美命]]（オオカモツミ）、[[久米氏]]の祖先の味耳命（ウマシミミ）、[[阿智祝部]]同族で[[知々夫国造]]の祖先の味耳命（ウマシミミ）、[[吉備氏]]の祖先の御鋤友耳命（ミスキトモミミ）、[[紀氏 (神別)|紀氏]]（[[紀国造]]）の祖先の豊耳命（トヨミミ）、肥君（火国造）の祖先の[[健緒組命]]（タケオグミ）、[[日置部氏]]の祖先の天櫛耳命（アメノクシミミ）、[[額田部氏]]の祖先の天由久富命（アメノユクトミ）がいる。
== 皇室系譜のミミおよびミ== [[皇室]]系譜の最も古い部分にミミおよびミの系譜が見られる。とりわけ[[天忍穂耳]]、[[神武天皇]]（別名ホホデミ）の子として多芸志美美命（[[手研耳命|手研耳（タギシミミ）命]]）、[[神八井耳命|神八井耳（カムヤイミミ）命]]、[[綏靖天皇|神渟名川耳（カムヌナカワミミ）尊]]、[[日子八井命|日子八井（ヒコヤイミミ）命]]（『日本書紀』には登場しない）、[[岐須美美命]]（『日本書紀』には登場しない。『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の[[研耳命|研耳（キスミミ）命]]に相当）が記録されている。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:みみ}} [[Category:日本神話]] [[Category:古代日本の称号]]

他に[[近江国]][[伊香郡]]の伊香刀美（イカトミ、[[伊香津臣命]]か）なども地名にミを付けた地域的首長を表すと考えられる。また[[綏靖天皇]]の和風諱号である神沼河耳命（カムヌナカワミミ）や、その兄である[[神八井耳命]]（カムヤイミミ）なども同様である。
== 神社におけるミミおよびミ == [[若狭国]]の[[彌美神社|彌美（ミミ）神社]]、[[信濃国]]の[[諏訪大社|南方刀美（ミナカタトミ）神社]]、[[近江国]]の[[阿志都弥神社|阿志都弥（アシツミ）神社]]、[[大和国]][[高市郡]]の[[加夜奈留美神社|加夜奈留美（カヤナルミ）神社]]、[[阿波国]][[美馬郡]]の[[波爾移麻比彌神社|波尓移麻比彌（ハニヤマヒミ）神社]]などは、ミミやミを神名に入れた神社である。
== 姓氏の中のミミおよびミ == [[氏]]の名の末尾にミ（ツミ）がつく代表的な例には、[[綿積豊玉彦命]]（ワダツミ）を祖先とする[[阿曇氏]]（アヅミ）や[[饒速日命]]を祖先とする[[穂積氏]]（ホヅミ）、[[和珥氏]]の同族と見られる鰐積氏（ワニヅミ）、[[出雲国造]]の同族と見られる出雲積氏（イヅモヅミ）、津積氏（ツヅミ）がある。
氏の名にミは付かないが、その祖先にミがつく例には、[[賀茂朝臣氏|賀茂氏]]の祖先の[[大賀茂都美命]]（オオカモツミ）、[[久米氏]]の祖先の味耳命（ウマシミミ）、[[阿智祝部]]同族で[[知々夫国造]]の祖先の味耳命（ウマシミミ）、[[吉備氏]]の祖先の御鋤友耳命（ミスキトモミミ）、[[紀氏 (神別)|紀氏]]（[[紀国造]]）の祖先の豊耳命（トヨミミ）、肥君（火国造）の祖先の[[健緒組命]]（タケオグミ）、[[日置部氏]]の祖先の天櫛耳命（アメノクシミミ）、[[額田部氏]]の祖先の天由久富命（アメノユクトミ）がいる。
== 皇室系譜のミミおよびミ== [[皇室]]系譜の最も古い部分にミミおよびミの系譜が見られる。とりわけ[[天忍穂耳]]、[[神武天皇]]（別名ホホデミ）の子として多芸志美美命（[[手研耳命|手研耳（タギシミミ）命]]）、[[神八井耳命|神八井耳（カムヤイミミ）命]]、[[綏靖天皇|神渟名川耳（カムヌナカワミミ）尊]]、[[日子八井命|日子八井（ヒコヤイミミ）命]]（『日本書紀』には登場しない）、[[岐須美美命]]（『日本書紀』には登場しない。『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の[[研耳命|研耳（キスミミ）命]]に相当）が記録されている。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:みみ}} [[Category:日本神話]] [[Category:古代日本の称号]]

“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog|archive-date=2023-01-26|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美}} and Mi (彌、見、美、海、看).
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
More people with it in their names are, Suga no Yatsumimi, {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|lt=Futimi no Kami}}, {{ill|Torimimi|ja|鳥取神}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神}} is also considered a regional chief.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
Some people have names historians think came from the place they ruled, plus Mi or Mimi. People like this include Ikatomi of {{ill|Ika District, Shiga|lt=Ika District|en}}, [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] (Ikatomi, perhaps {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命}} ). [[Emperor Suizei]] 's Japanese-style name, Kaunumakawamimi, and his older brother, [[Kamuyaimimi]] may come from places called Kaunumakawa and Kamuyai.
The leader of {{ill|Toumakoku|en}} was called Mimi.<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|last=第２版,世界大百科事典内言及|first=百科事典マイペディア,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典|title=投馬国とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%8A%95%E9%A6%AC%E5%9B%BD-104202|access-date=2022-06-21|website=コトバンク|language=ja}}</ref>
== Mimi and Mi at shrines ==
"Mimi" and "Mi" are also present in the names of several shrines across Japan such as the {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社}} in [[Wakasa Province]] and {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en}} in [[Shinano Province]]. * {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社}} in [[Wakasa Province]],
* {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en}} in [[Shinano Province]]
* {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine|ja|阿志都弥神社}} in [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] * {{ill|Kayanarumi Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美神社}} in Takaichi County, [[Yamato Province]] * {{ill|Haniyamahimi Shrine|ja|波爾移麻比彌神社}} in {{ill|Mima District, Tokushima|lt=Mima District|en}} [[Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]]
== Mimi and Mi in clans == Furthermore, these words appear in the names of ancient Japanese clans, these are some of those clans.
* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari}}. * {{ill|Wanizumi clan|ja|和珥氏}}, which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{ill|Kamo clan|en}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏}} and {{ill|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|知々夫国造}} - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏}} - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{ill|Nukatabe clan|ja|額田部氏}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en}}</ref>
* {{ill|Tagishimimi|en}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").
=== Genealogy === Note that [[Emperor Suizei]] went by the original name of ''Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto'' {{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki}}

The leader of {{ill|Toumakoku|en}} was called Mimi.<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|last=第２版,世界大百科事典内言及|first=百科事典マイペディア,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典|title=投馬国とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%8A%95%E9%A6%AC%E5%9B%BD-104202|access-date=2022-06-21|website=コトバンク|language=ja|qq=draft}}</ref>
== Mimi and Mi at shrines ==
"Mimi" and "Mi" are also present in the names of several shrines across Japan such as the {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft}} in [[Wakasa Province]] and {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft}} in [[Shinano Province]]. * {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft}} in [[Wakasa Province]],
* {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft}} in [[Shinano Province]]
* {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine|ja|阿志都弥神社|qq=draft}} in [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] * {{ill|Kayanarumi Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美神社|qq=draft}} in Takaichi County, [[Yamato Province]] * {{ill|Haniyamahimi Shrine|ja|波爾移麻比彌神社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Mima District, Tokushima|lt=Mima District|en|qq=draft}} [[Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]]
== Mimi and Mi in clans == Furthermore, these words appear in the names of ancient Japanese clans, these are some of those clans.
* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Wanizumi clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft}}, which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{ill|Kamo clan|en}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|知々夫国造|qq=draft}} - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft}} - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|qq=draft}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{ill|Nukatabe clan|ja|額田部氏|qq=draft}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en|qq=draft}}</ref>
* {{ill|Tagishimimi|en}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").
=== Genealogy === Note that [[Emperor Suizei]] went by the original name of ''Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto'' {{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki|qq=draft}}

[[simple:Mimi and Mi]] [[en:Mimi and Mi]] [[sn:Mimi and Mi]] [[fr:Mimi et Mi]] [[ja:ミミおよびミ]]
<!--Mimi and Mi-->

“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog|archive-date=2023-01-26|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|qq=draft}} and Mi (彌、見、美、海、看).
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
More people with it in their names are, Suga no Yatsumimi, {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|lt=Futimi no Kami|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Torimimi|ja|鳥取神|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=draft}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|qq=draft}} is also considered a regional chief.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
Some people have names historians think came from the place they ruled, plus Mi or Mimi. People like this include Ikatomi of {{ill|Ika District, Shiga|lt=Ika District|en|qq=draft}}, [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] (Ikatomi, perhaps {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|qq=draft}} ). [[Emperor Suizei]] 's Japanese-style name, Kaunumakawamimi, and his older brother, [[Kamuyaimimi]] may come from places called Kaunumakawa and Kamuyai.
The leader of {{ill|Toumakoku|en}} was called Mimi.<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|last=第２版,世界大百科事典内言及|first=百科事典マイペディア,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典|title=投馬国とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%8A%95%E9%A6%AC%E5%9B%BD-104202|access-date=2022-06-21|website=コトバンク|language=ja|qq=draft}}</ref>
== Mimi and Mi at shrines ==
"Mimi" and "Mi" are also present in the names of several shrines across Japan such as the {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft}} in [[Wakasa Province]] and {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft}} in [[Shinano Province]]. * {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft}} in [[Wakasa Province]],
* {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft}} in [[Shinano Province]]
* {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine|ja|阿志都弥神社|qq=draft}} in [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] * {{ill|Kayanarumi Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美神社|qq=draft}} in Takaichi County, [[Yamato Province]] * {{ill|Haniyamahimi Shrine|ja|波爾移麻比彌神社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Mima District, Tokushima|lt=Mima District|en|qq=draft}} [[Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]]
== Mimi and Mi in clans == Furthermore, these words appear in the names of ancient Japanese clans, these are some of those clans.
* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Wanizumi clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft}}, which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{ill|Kamo clan|en}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|知々夫国造|qq=draft}} - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft}} - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|qq=draft}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{ill|Nukatabe clan|ja|額田部氏|qq=draft}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en|qq=draft}}</ref>
* {{ill|Tagishimimi|en}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").

<!--Mimi and Mi-->

“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog|archive-date=2023-01-26|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|qq=draft}} and Mi (彌、見、美、海、看).
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|qq=}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
More people with it in their names are, Suga no Yatsumimi, {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|lt=Futimi no Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Torimimi|ja|鳥取神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|qq=}} is also considered a regional chief.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
Some people have names historians think came from the place they ruled, plus Mi or Mimi. People like this include Ikatomi of {{ill|Ika District, Shiga|lt=Ika District|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika District, Shiga|qq=}}, [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] (Ikatomi, perhaps {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} ). [[Emperor Suizei]] 's Japanese-style name, Kaunumakawamimi, and his older brother, [[Kamuyaimimi]] may come from places called Kaunumakawa and Kamuyai.
The leader of {{ill|Toumakoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toumakoku|qq=}} was called Mimi.<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|last=第２版,世界大百科事典内言及|first=百科事典マイペディア,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典|title=投馬国とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%8A%95%E9%A6%AC%E5%9B%BD-104202|access-date=2022-06-21|website=コトバンク|language=ja|qq=draft}}</ref>
== Mimi and Mi at shrines ==
"Mimi" and "Mi" are also present in the names of several shrines across Japan such as the {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Wakasa Province]] and {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|qq=}} in [[Shinano Province]]. * {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Wakasa Province]],
* {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|qq=}} in [[Shinano Province]]
* {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine|ja|阿志都弥神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashizumi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] * {{ill|Kayanarumi Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi Shrine|qq=}} in Takaichi County, [[Yamato Province]] * {{ill|Haniyamahimi Shrine|ja|波爾移麻比彌神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahimi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Mima District, Tokushima|lt=Mima District|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mima District, Tokushima|qq=}} [[Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]]
== Mimi and Mi in clans == Furthermore, these words appear in the names of ancient Japanese clans, these are some of those clans.
* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}. * {{ill|Wanizumi clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wanizumi clan|qq=}}, which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{ill|Kamo clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|知々夫国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi clan|qq=}} - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe clan|qq=}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{ill|Nukata clan|ja|額田部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe clan|qq=}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-oshihomimi|qq=}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en|qq=draft}}</ref>
* {{ill|Tagishimimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagishimimi|qq=}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").
=== Genealogy === Note that [[Emperor Suizei]] went by the original name of ''Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto'' {{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki|qq=draft}}
