In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|qq=}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
More people with it in their names are, Suga no Yatsumimi, {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|lt=Futimi no Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Torimimi|ja|鳥取神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|qq=}} is also considered a regional chief.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
Some people have names historians think came from the place they ruled, plus Mi or Mimi. People like this include Ikatomi of {{ill|Ika District, Shiga|lt=Ika District|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika District, Shiga|qq=}}, [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] (Ikatomi, perhaps {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} ). [[Emperor Suizei]] 's Japanese-style name, Kaunumakawamimi, and his older brother, [[Kamuyaimimi]] may come from places called Kaunumakawa and Kamuyai.
The leader of {{ill|Toumakoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toumakoku|qq=}} was called Mimi.<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|last=第２版,世界大百科事典内言及|first=百科事典マイペディア,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典|title=投馬国とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%8A%95%E9%A6%AC%E5%9B%BD-104202|access-date=2022-06-21|website=コトバンク|language=ja|qq=draft}}</ref>
== Mimi and Mi at shrines ==
"Mimi" and "Mi" are also present in the names of several shrines across Japan such as the {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Wakasa Province]] and {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|qq=}} in [[Shinano Province]]. * {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Wakasa Province]],
* {{ill|Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|qq=}} in [[Shinano Province]]
* {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine|ja|阿志都弥神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashizumi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] * {{ill|Kayanarumi Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi Shrine|qq=}} in Takaichi County, [[Yamato Province]] * {{ill|Haniyamahimi Shrine|ja|波爾移麻比彌神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahimi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Mima District, Tokushima|lt=Mima District|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mima District, Tokushima|qq=}} [[Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]]
== Mimi and Mi in clans == Furthermore, these words appear in the names of ancient Japanese clans, these are some of those clans.
* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}. * {{ill|Wanizumi clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wanizumi clan|qq=}}, which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{ill|Kamo clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|知々夫国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi clan|qq=}} - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe clan|qq=}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{ill|Nukata clan|ja|額田部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe clan|qq=}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-oshihomimi|qq=}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en|qq=draft}}</ref>
* {{ill|Tagishimimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagishimimi|qq=}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").
=== Genealogy === Note that [[Emperor Suizei]] went by the original name of ''Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto'' {{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki|qq=draft}}

[[simple:Mimi and Mi]] [[en:Mimi and Mi]] [[sn:Mimi and Mi]] [[fr:Mimi et Mi]] [[ja:ミミおよびミ]]
<!--Mimi and Mi-->

“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog|archive-date=2023-01-26|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|qq=draft}} and Mi (彌、見、美、海、看).
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|qq=}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
More people with it in their names are, Suga no Yatsumimi, {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|lt=Futimi no Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Torimimi|ja|鳥取神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|qq=}} is also considered a regional chief.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
Some people have names historians think came from the place they ruled, plus Mi or Mimi. People like this include Ikatomi of {{ill|Ika District, Shiga|lt=Ika District|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika District, Shiga|qq=}}, [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] (Ikatomi, perhaps {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} ). [[Emperor Suizei]] 's Japanese-style name, Kaunumakawamimi, and his older brother, [[Kamuyaimimi]] may come from places called Kaunumakawa and Kamuyai.
The leader of {{ill|Toumakoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toumakoku|qq=}} was called Mimi.<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|last=第２版,世界大百科事典内言及|first=百科事典マイペディア,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典|title=投馬国とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%8A%95%E9%A6%AC%E5%9B%BD-104202|access-date=2022-06-21|website=コトバンク|language=ja|qq=draft}}</ref>

* {{ill|Kamo clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|知々夫国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi clan|qq=}} - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe clan|qq=}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{ill|Nukata clan|ja|額田部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe clan|qq=}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} - Umashimimi

The leader of {{ill|Toumakoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toumakoku|qq=}} was called Mimi.<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|last=第２版,世界大百科事典内言及|first=百科事典マイペディア,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典|title=投馬国とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%8A%95%E9%A6%AC%E5%9B%BD-104202|access-date=2022-06-21|website=コトバンク|language=ja|qq=draft}}</ref>
== Mimi and Mi at shrines ==
"Mimi" and "Mi" are also present in the names of several shrines across Japan such as the {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Wakasa Province]] and {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|qq=}} in [[Shinano Province]]. * {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Wakasa Province]],
* {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|qq=}} in [[Shinano Province]]
* {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine|ja|阿志都弥神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashizumi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] * {{ill|Kayanarumi Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi Shrine|qq=}} in Takaichi County, [[Yamato Province]] * {{ill|Haniyamahimi Shrine|ja|波爾移麻比彌神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahimi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Mima District, Tokushima|lt=Mima District|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mima District, Tokushima|qq=}} [[Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]]
== Mimi and Mi in clans == Furthermore, these words appear in the names of ancient Japanese clans, these are some of those clans.
* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}. * {{ill|Wanizumi clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wanizumi clan|qq=}}, which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{ill|Kamo clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|知々夫国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi clan|qq=}} - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe clan|qq=}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{ill|Nukata clan|ja|額田部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe clan|qq=}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-oshihomimi|qq=}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en|qq=draft}}</ref>
* {{ill|Tagishimimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagishimimi|qq=}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").
=== Genealogy === Note that [[Emperor Suizei]] went by the original name of ''Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto'' {{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki|qq=draft}}

[[simple:Mimi and Mi]] [[en:Mimi and Mi]] [[sn:Mimi and Mi]] [[fr:Mimi et Mi]] [[ja:ミミおよびミ]]
<!--Mimi and Mi-->

“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog|archive-date=2023-01-26|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|qq=draft}} and Mi (彌、見、美、海、看).
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|qq=}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
More people with it in their names are, Suga no Yatsumimi, {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|lt=Futimi no Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|lt=Tottori-no-Kami|lt=Torimimi|ja|鳥取神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|qq=}} is also considered a regional chief.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
Some people have names historians think came from the place they ruled, plus Mi or Mimi. People like this include Ikatomi of {{ill|Ika District, Shiga|lt=Ika District|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika District, Shiga|qq=}}, [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] (Ikatomi, perhaps {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} ). [[Emperor Suizei]] 's Japanese-style name, Kaunumakawamimi, and his older brother, [[Kamuyaimimi]] may come from places called Kaunumakawa and Kamuyai.
The leader of {{ill|Toumakoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toumakoku|qq=}} was called Mimi.<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|last=第２版,世界大百科事典内言及|first=百科事典マイペディア,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典|title=投馬国とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%8A%95%E9%A6%AC%E5%9B%BD-104202|access-date=2022-06-21|website=コトバンク|language=ja|qq=draft}}</ref>
== Mimi and Mi at shrines ==
"Mimi" and "Mi" are also present in the names of several shrines across Japan such as the {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Wakasa Province]] and {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|qq=}} in [[Shinano Province]]. * {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Wakasa Province]],
* {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|qq=}} in [[Shinano Province]]
* {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine|ja|阿志都弥神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashizumi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] * {{ill|Kayanarumi Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi Shrine|qq=}} in Takaichi County, [[Yamato Province]] * {{ill|Haniyamahimi Shrine|ja|波爾移麻比彌神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahimi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Mima District, Tokushima|lt=Mima District|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mima District, Tokushima|qq=}} [[Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]]
== Mimi and Mi in clans == Furthermore, these words appear in the names of ancient Japanese clans, these are some of those clans.
* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}. * {{ill|Wanizumi clan|ja|和珥氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wanizumi clan|qq=}}, which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{ill|Kamo clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|知々夫国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi clan|qq=}} - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe clan|qq=}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{ill|Nukata clan|ja|額田部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe clan|qq=}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-oshihomimi|qq=}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en|qq=draft}}</ref>
* {{ill|Tagishimimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagishimimi|qq=}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").

"Mimi" and "Mi" are also present in the names of several shrines across Japan such as the {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Wakasa Province]] and {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|qq=}} in [[Shinano Province]]. * {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Wakasa Province]],
* {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|qq=}} in [[Shinano Province]]
* {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine|ja|阿志都弥神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashizumi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] * {{ill|Kayanarumi Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi Shrine|qq=}} in Takaichi County, [[Yamato Province]] * {{ill|Haniyamahimi Shrine|ja|波爾移麻比彌神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahimi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Mima District, Tokushima|lt=Mima District|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mima District, Tokushima|qq=}} [[Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]]
== Mimi and Mi in clans == Furthermore, these words appear in the names of ancient Japanese clans, these are some of those clans.
* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}. * [[:en:Wani clan|Wanizumi clan]], which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{ill|Kamo clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Chichibu Province|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe clan|qq=}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{ill|Nukata clan|ja|額田部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe clan|qq=}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-oshihomimi|qq=}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en|qq=draft}}</ref>
* {{ill|Tagishimimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagishimimi|qq=}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").
=== Genealogy === Note that [[Emperor Suizei]] went by the original name of ''Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto'' {{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki|qq=draft}}

revision.text:
“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog|archive-date=2023-01-26|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|qq=draft}} and Mi (彌、見、美、海、看).
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|qq=}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
More people with it in their names are, Suga no Yatsumimi, {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|lt=Futimi no Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|lt=Tottori-no-Kami|lt=Torimimi|ja|鳥取神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|qq=}} is also considered a regional chief.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
Some people have names historians think came from the place they ruled, plus Mi or Mimi. People like this include Ikatomi of {{ill|Ika District, Shiga|lt=Ika District|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika District, Shiga|qq=}}, [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] (Ikatomi, perhaps {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} ). [[Emperor Suizei]] 's Japanese-style name, Kaunumakawamimi, and his older brother, [[Kamuyaimimi]] may come from places called Kaunumakawa and Kamuyai.
The leader of {{ill|Toumakoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toumakoku|qq=}} was called Mimi.<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|last=第２版,世界大百科事典内言及|first=百科事典マイペディア,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典|title=投馬国とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%8A%95%E9%A6%AC%E5%9B%BD-104202|access-date=2022-06-21|website=コトバンク|language=ja|qq=draft}}</ref>
== Mimi and Mi at shrines ==
"Mimi" and "Mi" are also present in the names of several shrines across Japan such as the {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Wakasa Province]] and {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|qq=}} in [[Shinano Province]]. * {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Wakasa Province]],
* {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|qq=}} in [[Shinano Province]]
* {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine|ja|阿志都弥神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashizumi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] * {{ill|Kayanarumi Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi Shrine|qq=}} in Takaichi County, [[Yamato Province]] * {{ill|Haniyamahimi Shrine|ja|波爾移麻比彌神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahimi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Mima District, Tokushima|lt=Mima District|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mima District, Tokushima|qq=}} [[Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]]
== Mimi and Mi in clans == Furthermore, these words appear in the names of ancient Japanese clans, these are some of those clans.
* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}. * [[:en:Wani clan|Wanizumi clan]], which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{ill|Kamo clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Chichibu Province|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe clan|qq=}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{ill|Nukata clan|ja|額田部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe clan|qq=}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-oshihomimi|qq=}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en|qq=draft}}</ref>
* {{ill|Tagishimimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagishimimi|qq=}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").
=== Genealogy === Note that [[Emperor Suizei]] went by the original name of ''Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto'' {{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki|qq=draft}}

* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}. * [[:en:Wani clan|Wanizumi clan]], which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{ill|Kamo clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Chichibu Province|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe clan|qq=}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{ill|Nukata clan|ja|額田部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe clan|qq=}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-oshihomimi|qq=}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en|qq=draft}}</ref>
* {{ill|Tagishimimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagishimimi|qq=}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").
=== Genealogy === Note that [[Emperor Suizei]] went by the original name of ''Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto'' {{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki|qq=draft}}

[[simple:Mimi and Mi]] [[en:Mimi and Mi]] [[sn:Mimi and Mi]] [[fr:Mimi et Mi]] [[ja:ミミおよびミ]]
<!--Mimi and Mi--> [[Category:古代日本の称号]] [[Category:Japanese mythology]]

“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog|archive-date=2023-01-26|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|qq=draft}} and Mi (彌、見、美、海、看).
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|qq=}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
More people with it in their names are, Suga no Yatsumimi, {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|lt=Futimi no Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|lt=Tottori-no-Kami|lt=Torimimi|ja|鳥取神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|qq=}} is also considered a regional chief.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
Some people have names historians think came from the place they ruled, plus Mi or Mimi. People like this include Ikatomi of {{ill|Ika District, Shiga|lt=Ika District|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika District, Shiga|qq=}}, [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] (Ikatomi, perhaps {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} ). [[Emperor Suizei]] 's Japanese-style name, Kaunumakawamimi, and his older brother, [[Kamuyaimimi]] may come from places called Kaunumakawa and Kamuyai.
The leader of {{ill|Toumakoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toumakoku|qq=}} was called Mimi.<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|last=第２版,世界大百科事典内言及|first=百科事典マイペディア,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典|title=投馬国とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%8A%95%E9%A6%AC%E5%9B%BD-104202|access-date=2022-06-21|website=コトバンク|language=ja|qq=draft}}</ref>
== Mimi and Mi at shrines ==
"Mimi" and "Mi" are also present in the names of several shrines across Japan such as the {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Wakasa Province]] and {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|qq=}} in [[Shinano Province]]. * {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Wakasa Province]],
* {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|qq=}} in [[Shinano Province]]
* {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine|ja|阿志都弥神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashizumi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] * {{ill|Kayanarumi Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi Shrine|qq=}} in Takaichi County, [[Yamato Province]] * {{ill|Haniyamahimi Shrine|ja|波爾移麻比彌神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahimi Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Mima District, Tokushima|lt=Mima District|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mima District, Tokushima|qq=}} [[Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]]
== Mimi and Mi in clans == Furthermore, these words appear in the names of ancient Japanese clans, these are some of those clans.
* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}. * [[:en:Wani clan|Wanizumi clan]], which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{ill|Kamo clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Chichibu Province|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe clan|qq=}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{ill|Nukata clan|ja|額田部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe clan|qq=}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-oshihomimi|qq=}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en|qq=draft}}</ref>
* {{ill|Tagishimimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagishimimi|qq=}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").
=== Genealogy === Note that [[Emperor Suizei]] went by the original name of ''Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto'' {{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki|qq=draft}}

== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-oshihomimi|qq=}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en|qq=draft}}</ref>
* {{ill|Tagishimimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagishimimi|qq=}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").
=== Genealogy === Note that [[Emperor Suizei]] went by the original name of ''Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto'' {{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki|qq=draft}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
{{translated page|ja|ミミおよびミ|version=103153801|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[simple:Mimi and Mi]]
[[sn:Mimi and Mi]] [[fr:Mimi et Mi]] [[ja:ミミおよびミ]]
<!--Mimi and Mi--> [[Category:古代日本の称号]] [[Category:Japanese mythology]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog|archive-date=2023-01-26|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|qq=draft}} and Mi (彌、見、美、海、看).
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|qq=}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
More people with it in their names are, Suga no Yatsumimi, {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|lt=Futimi no Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|lt=Tottori-no-Kami|lt=Torimimi|ja|鳥取神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|qq=}} is also considered a regional chief.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
Some people have names historians think came from the place they ruled, plus Mi or Mimi. People like this include Ikatomi of {{ill|Ika District, Shiga|lt=Ika District|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika District, Shiga|qq=}}, [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] (Ikatomi, perhaps {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} ). [[Emperor Suizei]] 's Japanese-style name, Kaunumakawamimi, and his older brother, [[Kamuyaimimi]] may come from places called Kaunumakawa and Kamuyai.
The leader of {{ill|Toumakoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toumakoku|qq=}} was called Mimi.<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|last=第２版,世界大百科事典内言及|first=百科事典マイペディア,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典|title=投馬国とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%8A%95%E9%A6%AC%E5%9B%BD-104202|access-date=2022-06-21|website=コトバンク|language=ja|qq=draft}}</ref>
== Mimi and Mi at shrines ==
"Mimi" and "Mi" are also present in the names of several shrines across Japan such as the {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[Wakasa Province]] and {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|qq=}} in [[Shinano Province]]. * {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[Wakasa Province]],
* {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|qq=}} in [[Shinano Province]]
* {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine|ja|阿志都弥神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashizumi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] * {{ill|Kayanarumi Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Takaichi County, [[Yamato Province]] * {{ill|Haniyamahimi Shrine|ja|波爾移麻比彌神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahimi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Mima District, Tokushima|lt=Mima District|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mima District, Tokushima|qq=}} [[Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]]
== Mimi and Mi in clans == Furthermore, these words appear in the names of ancient Japanese clans, these are some of those clans.
* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}. * [[:en:Wani clan|Wanizumi clan]], which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{ill|Kamo clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Chichibu Province|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{ill|Nukata clan|ja|額田部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe clan|qq=}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-oshihomimi|qq=}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en|qq=draft}}</ref>
* {{ill|Tagishimimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagishimimi|qq=}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").
=== Genealogy === Note that [[Emperor Suizei]] went by the original name of ''Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto'' {{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki|qq=draft}}

* {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|qq=}} in [[Shinano Province]]
* {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine|ja|阿志都弥神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashizumi Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] * {{ill|Kayanarumi Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Takaichi County, [[Yamato Province]] * {{ill|Haniyamahimi Shrine|ja|波爾移麻比彌神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahimi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Mima District, Tokushima|lt=Mima District|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mima District, Tokushima|qq=}} [[Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]]
== Mimi and Mi in clans == Furthermore, these words appear in the names of ancient Japanese clans, these are some of those clans.
* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}. * [[:en:Wani clan|Wanizumi clan]], which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{ill|Kamo clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Chichibu Province|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{ill|Nukata clan|ja|額田部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe clan|qq=}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} - Umashimimi

* {{ill|Tagishimimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagishimimi|qq=}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").

revision.text:
“'''Mimi'''" and "'''Mi'''" are Japanese words that can be found in many names related to ancient Japan. They are very common in names of royal family members.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=魏志倭人伝、彌彌、彌彌那利|url=https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|access-date=2023-01-26|website=Forum_tokyoblog|archive-date=2023-01-26|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230126011011/https://tokyox.sakura.ne.jp/forum/discussion/49/%E9%AD%8F%E5%BF%97%E5%80%AD%E4%BA%BA%E4%BC%9D-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C-%E5%BD%8C%E5%BD%8C%E9%82%A3%E5%88%A9|url-status=dead}}</ref> They're also found in names of leaders, people, and objects. "Mi" can mean "divine spirit" and can be a part of names for deities. For example, the terms "[[Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|qq=draft}} and Mi (彌、見、美、海、看).
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|qq=}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
More people with it in their names are, Suga no Yatsumimi, {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|lt=Futimi no Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|lt=Tottori-no-Kami|lt=Torimimi|ja|鳥取神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|qq=}} is also considered a regional chief.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
Some people have names historians think came from the place they ruled, plus Mi or Mimi. People like this include Ikatomi of {{ill|Ika District, Shiga|lt=Ika District|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika District, Shiga|qq=}}, [[:en:Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] (Ikatomi, perhaps {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} ). [[Emperor Suizei]] 's Japanese-style name, Kaunumakawamimi, and his older brother, [[Kamuyaimimi]] may come from places called Kaunumakawa and Kamuyai.
The leader of {{ill|Toumakoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toumakoku|qq=}} was called Mimi.<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|last=第２版,世界大百科事典内言及|first=百科事典マイペディア,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典|title=投馬国とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%8A%95%E9%A6%AC%E5%9B%BD-104202|access-date=2022-06-21|website=コトバンク|language=ja|qq=draft}}</ref>
== Mimi and Mi at shrines ==
"Mimi" and "Mi" are also present in the names of several shrines across Japan such as the {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[Wakasa Province]] and {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|qq=}} in [[Shinano Province]]. * {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[Wakasa Province]],
* {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|qq=}} in [[Shinano Province]]
* {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine|ja|阿志都弥神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashizumi Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] * {{ill|Kayanarumi Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Takaichi County, [[Yamato Province]] * {{ill|Haniyamahimi Shrine|ja|波爾移麻比彌神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahimi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Mima District, Tokushima|lt=Mima District|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mima District, Tokushima|qq=}} [[:en:Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]]
== Mimi and Mi in clans == Furthermore, these words appear in the names of ancient Japanese clans, these are some of those clans.
* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}. * [[:en:Wani clan|Wanizumi clan]], which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{ill|Kamo clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Chichibu Province|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{ill|Nukata clan|ja|額田部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe clan|qq=}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-oshihomimi|qq=}} the grandfather of [[Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en|qq=draft}}</ref>
* {{ill|Tagishimimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagishimimi|qq=}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").
=== Genealogy === Note that [[Emperor Suizei]] went by the original name of ''Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto'' {{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki|qq=draft}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
{{translated page|ja|ミミおよびミ|version=103153801|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Completion tier 4]]
}}

* {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|qq=}} in [[Shinano Province]]

* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}. * [[:en:Wani clan|Wanizumi clan]], which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{ill|Kamo clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Chichibu Province|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{ill|Nukata clan|ja|額田部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe clan|qq=}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} - Umashimimi

* {{ill|Tagishimimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagishimimi|qq=}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").

For example, the terms "[[Watatsumi]]" and "{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}" mean "sea spirit" and "mountain spirit" respectively and both use the version 霊.<ref name=":1">[[本居宣長]]『[[古事記伝]]』七之巻。</ref>
Spellings include {{nihongo|Mimi|ミミ、耳、彌彌、美美|qq=draft}} and Mi (彌、見、美、海、看).
In ancient Japanese historical texts like "[[Kojiki]]" and "[[Nihon Shoki|Nihonshoki]]", people with "Mi" or "Mimi" in their names were often leaders in their regions. For instance, "{{ill|Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto|qq=}}" ruled in the [[Izumi Province|Izumi]] and [[Settsu Province|Settsu regions]], "Kugamimi" ruled in the [[Tamba Province|Tamba region]], and "Masakitsumimi" ruled in the [[Tajima Province|Tajima region]].<ref name=":2">溝口睦子「記紀神話解釈の一つのこころみ」『文学』1973rd year - 1974th year 。波多都美は[[Izumo Province ]][[飯石郡]][[波多郷]]の首長、支自麻都美はIzumo Province 飯石郡[[来島郷]]の首長である。</ref> and minor chiefs named Hatatsumi and Kijimatsumi are mentioned.<ref name=":2" />
More people with it in their names are, Suga no Yatsumimi, {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|lt=Futimi no Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tottori-no-Kami|lt=Tottori-no-Kami|lt=Torimimi|ja|鳥取神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimimi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|ja|多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|10=zh|11=多比理岐志麻流美神|1=Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahiriki-Shimarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}, who appear in the Izumo myth of the [[Kojiki]]. {{ill|Ame-no-Hibaraooshinadomi-no-Kami|ja|天日腹大科度美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohibara Oshinomi no Kami|qq=}} is also considered a regional chief.<ref>『古事記伝』による。須賀之八耳は[[須賀郷]]ないしは[[須佐郷]]、[[美呂浪神]]は[[美呂郷]]、[[布忍富鳥鳴海神]]は[[布師郷]]のミ（首長）を表す。</ref>
Some people have names historians think came from the place they ruled, plus Mi or Mimi. People like this include Ikatomi of {{ill|Ika District, Shiga|lt=Ika District|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ika District, Shiga|qq=}}, [[:en:Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] (Ikatomi, perhaps {{ill|Igatsuomi no Mikoto|ja|伊賀津臣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igatsuomi no Mikoto|qq=}} ). [[:en:Emperor Suizei|Emperor Suizei]] 's Japanese-style name, Kaunumakawamimi, and his older brother, [[Kamuyaimimi]] may come from places called Kaunumakawa and Kamuyai.
The leader of {{ill|Toumakoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toumakoku|qq=}} was called Mimi.<ref name=":02">{{Cite web|last=第２版,世界大百科事典内言及|first=百科事典マイペディア,日本大百科全書(ニッポニカ),ブリタニカ国際大百科事典 小項目事典,世界大百科事典|title=投馬国とは|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%8A%95%E9%A6%AC%E5%9B%BD-104202|access-date=2022-06-21|website=コトバンク|language=ja|qq=draft}}</ref>
== Mimi and Mi at shrines ==
"Mimi" and "Mi" are also present in the names of several shrines across Japan such as the {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[Wakasa Province]] and {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|qq=}} in [[Shinano Province]]. * {{ill|Mimi Shrine|ja|彌美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[Wakasa Province]],
* {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha|lt=Minakatatomi Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|qq=}} in [[Shinano Province]]
* {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine|ja|阿志都弥神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashizumi Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Omi Province]] * {{ill|Kayanarumi Shrine|ja|加夜奈留美神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayanarumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Takaichi County, [[Yamato Province]] * {{ill|Haniyamahimi Shrine|ja|波爾移麻比彌神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahimi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Mima District, Tokushima|lt=Mima District|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mima District, Tokushima|qq=}} [[:en:Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province]]
== Mimi and Mi in clans == Furthermore, these words appear in the names of ancient Japanese clans, these are some of those clans.
* [[Azumi people|Azumi clan]] whose [[Oyagami]] was [[Watatsumi]] * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} whose [[Oyagami]] was {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|en|Amenohoakari|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}. * [[:en:Wani clan|Wanizumi clan]], which is considered to be of the same family as the Wani clan * Izumozumi clan, which is considered to be of the same family as the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Tsuzumi clan (津積氏).
Here are some clans without the sound in their names, but their [[Oyagami|ancestors]] have it in their names
* {{ill|Kamo clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} - {{ill|Okamotsumi|ja|大鴨積命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okamotsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Achi clan|ja|阿智氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Chichibu Province|Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] - Uma-shimi -no-mikoto, * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] - Misuki-tomo-mimi, * {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} - Toyomimi-no-mikoto. * {{ill|Hiokibe clan|ja|日置部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiokibe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Ame no Kushimi, * {{ill|Nukata clan|ja|額田部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatabe clan|qq=}} - Ame no Yukutomi. * {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume clan|qq=}} - Umashimimi
== Mimi and Mi of Imperial Family Lineage == Mimi and Mi's lineage can be seen in the names of people in the Japanese royal family. Here are some of these people.
* {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-oshihomimi|qq=}} the grandfather of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sb3sCwAAQBAJ&q=oshihomimi&pg=PT45|title=Japanese Mythology: Hermeneutics on Scripture|last1=Isomae|first1=Jun'ichi|last2=Subramanian|first2=Mukund|date=2016-04-08|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-134-94908-3|language=en|qq=draft}}</ref>
* {{ill|Tagishimimi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagishimimi|qq=}} * [[Kamuyaimimi]] * [[:en:Emperor Suizei|Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto]] the Japanese name of Emperor Suizei * [[Hikoyai|Hikohachi Hikoyaimimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki"), * [[Kisumimi|Kisumimi no Mikoto]] (does not appear in "Nihonshoki").
=== Genealogy === Note that [[:en:Emperor Suizei|Emperor Suizei]] went by the original name of ''Kamununakawamimi no Mikoto'' {{A genealogical tree based on the Kojiki|qq=draft}}
